ab: ai makalaleketa ko ki’elraelra mosowa?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: eene, aikitoma.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: ai makalaleketa ko ki’elaelra mosowa？
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: ini, kwani tathingala neli bekelay kadro makalra.
en: B:Don't, I don't know much about it.

ab: lo amiyaobonga ‘waocogo so? naw mwa ‘olridraw amiya ko mosowa?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I'd like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: eene, na ikainga naiyana kilalava mosowa.
en: B: Okay, I'll wait for you at home.

ab: ‘waocogo so loza? ai ki’elaelra ko mosowa idra sindrwadroma sa mawlay ki soTaromak ka NgodraDrekai.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於魯凱族的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I'd like to ask you about the legendary story of the [Amis]. *Please change [Amis] to your respective tribe.

ab: momaoma ko loza, kadrowa ko ikai daane.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I'm going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won't be home.

ab: wa kiyalalra ko wathingale so makalra ki sidrwadromana kakoza ki soTaromak amiya mosawa， wakidramedrema ko ki‘elaelra mosowa.
ch: A:我聽說您對魯凱族的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of the Amis, so I would like to ask you a few questions. *Please change [Amis] to your ethnic group.

ab: kai tathingala neli kadro makalra, na ‘alraingi tobale kwadra tathingala neli. .
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don't know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: kalaleketa ko sasiyasing? loiniya ma’adolowa ko ‘acase si kiroko.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: eene, aikatoma.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: ‘iyanga kai cayli so?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: tara mabangatenga si valro kai cayli li.
en: B: I'm 98 years old.

ab: ‘iya walolrayana neso.
en: A: You're so young.

ab: ini, marodrangngana ko.
en: B: No, I'm not! I'm old.

ab: twalai so ino?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: twali zako Taromak.
en: B: I'm from [Matthew's Saddle]. *Please change [Matthew Saddle] to an ethnic location.

ab: ko sakacekele so miya ka so Taromak?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是魯凱族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also [Amis]? *Please change "Amis" to your ethnic group.

ab: ini, Swa’ariva.
en: B: No, she is a [Puyuma]. *[Puyuma] is freely interchangeable with other ethnic groups

ab: wathingale so mawriva swainino ka vaga?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: wathingala ko sinTaromaka si So’ariva.
ch: B: 我會說魯凱語和排灣語。
en: B: I can speak [Ami] and [Puyuma]. *Please change [Ami] [Puyuma] to the language you each speak.

ab: kaivayana matiyaragay kalaleketa ko ki’elaelra mosowa, la sosowa manema ka twalisiya ki tara lro’o?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It's a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: eene, sa lolrayana ko tara mobola ko walro’o.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: maelanenga, kai tabolroa neso kaivaiyana matiyadalame ‘aramoro.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: kai takawriva neli ‘atengatenga kai vaga, sinkadrwadromana tasosowa ki laomo si tarwarodranga.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: ta akela kikai kai takibolrowa neli, maelanenga kai ta’elaelrane so nakowa.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That's all for today's interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: sikaino, tamaceecelenga makalaelangenga..
en: B: You're welcome, let's talk again.

ab: ai davacengana ko, lo bilrilinganenga ai kilalranga ko makalra ki mwalay.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I'm going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: eene, anilalranga.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
